zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Matthews於1623年12月27日出生在英國英格蘭戴文聖 瑪麗 奧特利時，她的父親Francis Matthews25歲，母親Thamsine Channon25歲。她於1647年與William Drew在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1710年8月在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德多福 姆姆享年86歲，埋葬在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德達勒姆德魯 加里森 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Matthews于1623年12月27日出生在英国英格兰戴文聖 瑪麗 奧特利时，她的父亲Francis Matthews25岁，母亲Thamsine Channon25岁。她于1647年与William Drew在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1710年8月在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德多福 姆姆享年86岁，埋葬在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德達勒姆德魯 加里森 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Matthews
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (Ulster and County Louth): patronymic from the personal name Matthew . In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognates from other languages, such as German Matthäus (from the personal name Matthäus, from Latin Matthaeus) and Slovenian Matavž (from an obsolete vernacular form of the personal name Matevž, from Latin Matthaeus). Compare Mathews .
Irish: Anglicized form of Mac Mathghamhna (see McMahon ).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Matthews was born on 27 December 1623, in Saint Mary Ottery, Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, Francis Matthews, was 25 and her mother, Thamsine Channon, was 25. She married William Drew in 1647, in New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in August 1710, in Dover MM, Strafford, New Hampshire, United States, at the age of 86, and was buried in Drew Garrison Cemetery, Durham, Strafford, New Hampshire, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Matthews(이)가 1623년 12월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 세인트 메리 오터이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Francis Matthews(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Thamsine Channon(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1647년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르에서 William Drew(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1710년 8월 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 도버에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 뒤람 드루 개리슨 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Matthews nasceu em 27 de dezembro de 1623, em Saint Mary Ottery, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Francis Matthews, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Thamsine Channon, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com William Drew em 1647, em New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em agosto de 1710, em Dover MM, Strafford, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América, com 86 anos, e foi sepultada em Drew Garrison Cemetery, Durham, Strafford, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Matthews родилась 27 декабря 1623 г. в местности сеинт мэри оттири, девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Francis Matthews было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Thamsine Channon, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Drew в 1647 г. в местности ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла август 1710 г. в местности довир мм, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 86 лет и была похоронена в местности дрю гаррисон кеметири, дурам, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты.

Se casó con William Drew en 1647, en New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en agosto de 1710, en Dover MM, Strafford, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultada en Drew Garrison Cemetery, Durham, Strafford, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos.
